pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 12, 18, 99 and export_result.sh line 9; task.json description; .project file; verifier.py and export_result.sh confirm project directory path

Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 82 creates jar with this exact name; .classpath initial content line 154 references 'lib/dose-engine.jar'; verifier.py line 68 searches for 'dose-engine.jar' in path attribute
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 90 creates zip with this exact name; verifier.py line 27 expects this name from metadata; verifier.py line 84 checks if this name is in sourcepath attribute
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 13 defines DOWNLOADS=/home/ga/Downloads; line 90 creates zip at $DOWNLOADS/dose-engine-src.zip; export_result.sh would reference this path for verification

Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 27-71 create DoseCalculator.java with package com.dosimetry.engine (line 30) and class DoseCalculator (line 38); verifier.py line 104 references this class in task description

privileged_info_summary: The TreatmentPlanner project contains a library dependency on dose-engine.jar (compiled from synthetic Java source). The library defines the com.dosimetry.engine.DoseCalculator class with methods for calculating Monitor Units in radiotherapy dosimetry. The source archive dose-engine-src.zip contains the original Java source code (DoseCalculator.java) and is located at /home/ga/Downloads/dose-engine-src.zip. All metadata claims about project name, jar name, source zip name, source zip path, and expected class name are correct and consistent with the actual generated data.
